req.title: Approve leave request — variant 4
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path approval by the assigned manager — EP on valid actor/state; (2) rejection by a non-assignee manager — NEG/403 path; (3) double-action on an already-approved request — STC 'approved→approved' transition producing 409; (4) rate-limit boundary — BVA at the exact threshold where the (N+1)th request within the window must be blocked; (5) decision-table coverage of the approve/reject × assignee/non-assignee matrix. Together these provide EP, BVA, STC, DT, and NEG heuristic coverage as required by ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4.

tests.name: test_manager_approves_assigned_request_succeeds
preconditions: Leave request exists with state=pending and is assigned to this manager
Employee account exists and can receive notifications
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log for the request ID
expected: Leave request state transitions to approved
Audit entry exists with actor=manager, action=approve, request_id={id}
trace: R-WEB-065: manager clicks approve → request state=approved
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_approves_assigned_request_succeeds(
    api_client,          # <fixture> authenticated as manager
    pending_leave_request,  # <fixture> returns {id, employee_id}
    audit_log_query,     # <helper_fn>(request_id) -> list[dict]
    notification_query,  # <helper_fn>(employee_id) -> list[dict]
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    emp_id = pending_leave_request["employee_id"]

    resp = api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve")
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    leave = api_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert leave["state"] == "approved"

    notes = notification_query(emp_id)
    assert any(n["request_id"] == req_id for n in notes)

    entries = audit_log_query(req_id)
    assert any(
        e["action"] == "approve" and e["actor_role"] == "manager"
        for e in entries
    )

tests.name: test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager but is NOT the assignee of the request
Leave request exists with state=pending assigned to a different manager
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve with credentials of a different manager
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating not-assignee
No audit entry for approve action created
trace: R-WEB-065 error pathway: 403 if manager not the assignee
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403(
    api_client_factory,      # <helper_fn>(role, user_id) -> client
    pending_leave_request,   # <fixture> assigned to manager_A
    other_manager_credentials,  # <fixture> returns manager_B creds
    audit_log_query,         # <helper_fn>(request_id) -> list[dict]
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    client_b = api_client_factory("manager", other_manager_credentials["id"])

    resp = client_b.post(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve")
    assert resp.status_code == 403

    error_body = resp.json()
    assert "not_assignee" in error_body.get("error_code", "").lower() \
        or "forbidden" in error_body.get("message", "").lower()

    leave = client_b.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert leave["state"] == "pending"

    entries = audit_log_query(req_id)
    assert not any(e["action"] == "approve" for e in entries)

tests.name: test_approve_already_approved_request_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager and is the assignee
Leave request already has state=approved (previously actioned)
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve a second time with the same manager credentials
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body indicates request already actioned
Leave request state remains approved (no state regression)
trace: R-WEB-065 error pathway: 409 if already actioned
R-WEB-065 state transition: approved → approve-again is invalid
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_approve_already_approved_request_returns_409(
    api_client,               # <fixture> authenticated as assignee manager
    approved_leave_request,   # <fixture> returns {id} with state=approved
):
    req_id = approved_leave_request["id"]

    resp = api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve")
    assert resp.status_code == 409

    error_body = resp.json()
    assert "already" in error_body.get("message", "").lower() \
        or "conflict" in error_body.get("error_code", "").lower()

    leave = api_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert leave["state"] == "approved"

tests.name: test_rate_limit_exceeded_on_approve_endpoint
preconditions: Rate-limit policy for /leave-requests/*/approve is known (e.g. N requests per window)
N distinct pending leave requests assigned to this manager exist
Rate-limit counter for this manager is at zero (fresh window)
actions: Send exactly RATE_LIMIT_MAX approve requests (one per distinct request ID) — all should succeed
Send one additional (RATE_LIMIT_MAX + 1) approve request within the same window
Capture the status code of the final request
expected: First RATE_LIMIT_MAX requests each return 200 or 204
The (RATE_LIMIT_MAX + 1)th request returns 429 Too Many Requests
trace: R-WEB-065: operation must be rate-limited per user
BVA: exactly at limit (N) passes; one beyond (N+1) is blocked
tests.executable:
import pytest

RATE_LIMIT_MAX = 10  # <config_constant> read from env/config

def test_rate_limit_exceeded_on_approve_endpoint(
    api_client,                     # <fixture> authenticated as manager
    bulk_pending_requests_factory,  # <helper_fn>(n) -> list of request IDs
    reset_rate_limit,               # <fixture> ensures clean window before test
):
    request_ids = bulk_pending_requests_factory(RATE_LIMIT_MAX + 1)

    for req_id in request_ids[:RATE_LIMIT_MAX]:
        r = api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve")
        assert r.status_code in (200, 204), \
            f"Expected success for request {req_id}, got {r.status_code}"

    overflow_id = request_ids[RATE_LIMIT_MAX]
    resp = api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{overflow_id}/approve")
    assert resp.status_code == 429
    assert "retry-after" in (k.lower() for k in resp.headers)

tests.name: test_approve_reject_decision_matrix_assignee_vs_non_assignee
preconditions: Two managers exist: manager_A (assignee) and manager_B (non-assignee)
Two pending leave requests exist, both assigned to manager_A
Decision conditions: actor ∈ {assignee, non-assignee} × action ∈ {approve, reject}
actions: manager_B attempts to approve request_1 (already approved) — expect 403 or 409
manager_B attempts to reject request_2 (already rejected) — expect 403 or 409
expected: manager_B approve on request not assigned to them → 403
manager_B reject  on request not assigned to them → 403
trace: R-WEB-065 error pathway: 403 if manager not the assignee
DT: 2 actors × 2 actions = 4 decision rules fully exercised
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_approve_reject_decision_matrix_assignee_vs_non_assignee(
    api_client_factory,         # <helper_fn>(role, user_id) -> client
    manager_a_credentials,      # <fixture>
    manager_b_credentials,      # <fixture>
    two_pending_requests,       # <fixture> [{id, assignee_id}, ...] assigned to A
):
    client_a = api_client_factory("manager", manager_a_credentials["id"])
    client_b = api_client_factory("manager", manager_b_credentials["id"])
    r1, r2 = two_pending_requests[0]["id"], two_pending_requests[1]["id"]

    resp_approve = client_a.post(f"/leave-requests/{r1}/approve")
    assert resp_approve.status_code in (200, 204)
    assert client_a.get(f"/leave-requests/{r1}").json()["state"] == "approved"

    resp_reject = client_a.post(f"/leave-requests/{r2}/reject")
    assert resp_reject.status_code in (200, 204)
    assert client_a.get(f"/leave-requests/{r2}").json()["state"] == "rejected"

    assert client_b.post(f"/leave-requests/{r1}/approve").status_code in (403, 409)
    assert client_b.post(f"/leave-requests/{r2}/reject").status_code in (403, 409)
